explicit_text: I grew up in a quiet country village in Yamanashi Prefecture, near the foot of Mount Fuji. Many people from Yamanashi, especially from Kōfu on the other side of the mountain range, came to America. When I arrived here, most people from Yamanashi Prefecture lived in big cities like San Francisco and Los Angeles. My father came to America for financial reasons, leaving his wife and children behind in Japan initially.
implicit_text: I grew up in a quiet country village near the foot of Japan's tallest mountain. Many people from that region, especially from the city on the other side of the mountain range, came to America. When I arrived here, most people from that area lived in big cities like San Francisco and Los Angeles. My father came to America for financial reasons, leaving his wife and children behind in Japan initially.

explicit_text: I lived near the foot of Mount Fuji in Yamanashi Prefecture. Many people from around Mount Fuji, including those from Kōfu on the other side of the mountain range, emigrated to America. The mountain was a significant landmark in my home region, and it shaped the community from which many of us came. It was peaceful when I was little, and nothing special happened around the mountain during my childhood.

explicit_text: Kōfu is a city on the other side of the mountain range from where I lived near Mount Fuji. Many people from Kōfu came to America, just like those from my village. It was one of the main places in Yamanashi Prefecture that contributed to the Japanese community in big American cities. I heard that many from Kōfu settled in places like San Francisco and Los Angeles.
implicit_text: The city on the other side of the mountain range from where I lived near Japan's tallest peak was a place many people came to America from, just like those from my village. It was one of the main places in that prefecture that contributed to the Japanese community in big American cities. I heard that many from that city settled in places like San Francisco and Los Angeles.

explicit_text: I arrived in San Francisco after stopping overnight in Hawaii. My father came to meet me there, and we stayed overnight before going to Sacramento. I thought San Francisco was a beautiful place with many hills, which I noticed from the ship. Later, I transported vegetables from here to San Francisco by truck, making daily trips that took about four hours each way.
implicit_text: I arrived in the city known for its hills after stopping overnight in Hawaii. My father came to meet me there, and we stayed overnight before going to the state capital. I thought this city was beautiful, which I noticed from the ship. Later, I transported vegetables from nearby areas to this city by truck, making daily trips that took about four hours each way.

explicit_text: Many people from Yamanashi Prefecture lived in Los Angeles when I came to America. I also transported lettuce from Imperial Valley to San Francisco, passing through Los Angeles. When Ambassador Sato became a consul in Los Angeles, my wife resigned from her service and married me. Los Angeles was part of the route for our trucking business, and some of our trucks were repaired there.
implicit_text: Many people from the rural prefecture lived in the southern California city when I came to America. I also transported lettuce from a desert valley to the northern California city, passing through this southern city. When the Japanese diplomat became a consul in this city, my wife resigned from her service and married me. This city was part of the route for our trucking business, and some of our trucks were repaired there.

explicit_text: I sailed to America aboard the ship called Korea Maru, which took about two weeks. On board, I remember seeing many picture brides, though I was only 16 and did not speak to them. The journey was long, and after arriving in Hawaii, I stayed overnight at a hotel before continuing to San Francisco.
implicit_text: I sailed to America aboard a ship that took about two weeks. On board, I remember seeing many picture brides, though I was only 16 and did not speak to them. The journey was long, and after arriving in Hawaii, I stayed overnight at a hotel before continuing to the northern California city.

explicit_text: Before leaving Japan, I saw many picture brides at a hotel in Yokohama. It was a place where many immigrants gathered before their journey to America. I was only 16 at the time and did not have much interaction with them. Yokohama was an important port city for our departure.
implicit_text: Before leaving Japan, I saw many picture brides at a hotel in the major port city near Tokyo. It was a place where many immigrants gathered before their journey to America. I was only 16 at the time and did not have much interaction with them. This port city was an important departure point for us.

explicit_text: I landed in Hawaii and stayed there overnight during my journey to America. I remember thinking it was a hot place and stayed at a hotel named Kome-Ya. Hawaii was a brief stop before I continued on to San Francisco to meet my father. It was part of the long voyage on the Korea Maru.
implicit_text: I landed in the tropical islands and stayed there overnight during my journey to America. I remember thinking it was a hot place and stayed at a hotel named Kome-Ya. This stop was brief before I continued on to the northern California city to meet my father. It was part of the long voyage on the ship I traveled on.
cues: stopover en route to San Francisco

explicit_text: While in Hawaii, I stayed at a hotel called Kome-Ya. It was during my brief stopover on the way to San Francisco. The hotel was a place where I rested before continuing my journey to meet my father. I remember it because Hawaii was very hot compared to Japan.
implicit_text: While in the tropical islands, I stayed at a hotel called Kome-Ya. It was during my brief stopover on the way to the northern California city. The hotel was a place where I rested before continuing my journey to meet my father. I remember it because the islands were very hot compared to Japan.

explicit_text: After arriving in San Francisco, I went with my father to Sacramento, where we stayed for a while. I went to school there and learned some English. We lived in nearby Fair Oaks and later moved to West Sacramento to farm tomatoes. Sacramento was an important place for me to reconnect with my father after several years apart.
implicit_text: After arriving in the northern California city, I went with my father to the state capital, where we stayed for a while. I went to school there and learned some English. We lived in a nearby suburb and later moved to another suburb to farm tomatoes. That capital city was an important place for me to reconnect with my father after several years apart.

explicit_text: We lived in Fair Oaks after coming to America, and I helped my father with farming. The hills there were not as steep as those in Japan, so I thought it was a nice place. Fair Oaks has since become part of the city, and I can hardly imagine where we used to live. It was our first farming location in America.
implicit_text: We lived in a suburb after coming to America, and I helped my father with farming. The hills there were not as steep as those in Japan, so I thought it was a nice place. That suburb has since become part of the larger city, and I can hardly imagine where we used to live. It was our first farming location in America.

explicit_text: After Fair Oaks, we moved to West Sacramento and stayed there for about two to three years. We grew tomatoes during that time. It was part of our farming journey before moving to Salinas and eventually buying land for grape cultivation. West Sacramento was an important step in our agricultural life in America.
implicit_text: After the first suburb, we moved to another suburb in the same metropolitan area and stayed there for about two to three years. We grew tomatoes during that time. It was part of our farming journey before moving to a coastal city and eventually buying land for grape cultivation. That suburb was an important step in our agricultural life in America.

explicit_text: We moved to Salinas after West Sacramento and grew strawberries there for about two to three years. I don't remember all the details clearly, but it was part of our farming experience before settling permanently. Salinas was a place where we continued to work hard in agriculture. It was during the early 1920s after I came to America in 1918.
implicit_text: We moved to a coastal city after living in the suburbs and grew strawberries there for about two to three years. I don't remember all the details clearly, but it was part of our farming experience before settling permanently. That city was a place where we continued to work hard in agriculture. It was during the early 1920s after I came to America in 1918.

explicit_text: Mr. Abiko had already bought Yamato Colony in Livingston, California, and he started a sister colony where we bought land. Because Japanese immigrants could not own or lease land easily, we bought 20 acres in the colony in our children's names. Yamato Colony was a model for Japanese agricultural settlement and helped us establish a permanent home in America.
implicit_text: A community leader had already bought a Japanese immigrant agricultural settlement in a Central Valley town, and he started a sister settlement where we bought land. Because Japanese immigrants could not own or lease land easily, we bought 20 acres in the colony in our children's names. That colony was a model for Japanese agricultural settlement and helped us establish a permanent home in America.

explicit_text: Yamato Colony is located in Livingston, California, where Mr. Abiko had bought land. It served as a sister colony to the land we purchased for grape growing. Livingston was an example of Japanese immigrant agricultural communities in California. It influenced our decision to settle and farm permanently in the area.
implicit_text: The Japanese immigrant agricultural settlement is located in a Central Valley town where a community leader had bought land. It served as a sister colony to the land we purchased for grape growing. That town was an example of Japanese immigrant agricultural communities in California. It influenced our decision to settle and farm permanently in the area.

explicit_text: I bought a truck to transport vegetables from here to San Francisco, and it was one of the first trucks to use air in the tires, a new feature introduced by Dodge Motor Company. Before that, trucks only had rubber tires, so this was a big improvement. I commuted daily, driving at night to deliver vegetables and returning in the morning. The trucking business was hard work but helped support my family.
implicit_text: I bought a truck to transport vegetables from our area to the northern California city, and it was one of the first trucks to use air in the tires, a new feature introduced by a major American automobile company. Before that, trucks only had rubber tires, so this was a big improvement. I commuted daily, driving at night to deliver vegetables and returning in the morning. The trucking business was hard work but helped support my family.

explicit_text: After getting married, we moved to Pismo Beach. I continued the trucking business there, transporting lettuce from Imperial Valley to San Francisco. Four partners organized the trucking company, and white drivers often drove the trucks. Pismo Beach was part of our business route and home for a time before the war broke out.
implicit_text: After getting married, we moved to a coastal town. I continued the trucking business there, transporting lettuce from a desert valley to the northern California city. Four partners organized the trucking company, and white drivers often drove the trucks. That coastal town was part of our business route and home for a time before the war broke out.

explicit_text: I lived in Oakland for less than a year before moving to Pismo Beach. It was during the time I was looking for my wife, who was still in Japan. Oakland was a brief stop in my journey before settling down and starting a family. It was part of my early years in America after arriving in 1918.
implicit_text: I lived in a major East Bay city for less than a year before moving to the coastal town. It was during the time I was looking for my wife, who was still in Japan. That city was a brief stop in my journey before settling down and starting a family. It was part of my early years in America after arriving in 1918.

explicit_text: My wife came to America as a maid to Ambassador Sato in Washington, D.C. She worked there for three years before marrying me. It was a big change for her, coming from the country in Japan to work in a high-ranking diplomat's family in the U.S. capital. She sometimes cried missing her parents but eventually got used to life there.
implicit_text: My wife came to America as a maid to a Japanese diplomat in the U.S. capital. She worked there for three years before marrying me. She sometimes cried missing her parents but eventually got used to life there.

explicit_text: I married Cortez Kajiwara, who was from a family connected to mine through marriage ties. When he was 16, he stopped by my house in Japan, though I was only 10 then. Many people helped me come to America, and I was summoned here as a maid before marrying him. We lived together in Pismo Beach and had children before the war broke out.

explicit_text: I transported lettuce from Imperial Valley to San Francisco as part of my trucking business. The route included stops in Los Angeles and Pismo Beach. The work was hard and involved long hours, but it provided a steady income. Imperial Valley was an important agricultural area for our business.

explicit_text: Sometimes our trucks picked up artichokes from Davenport and took them to Los Angeles. It was part of the diverse vegetable transportation work we did. Davenport was one of the agricultural points along our trucking routes. This work was part of the trucking business I ran with partners.

explicit_text: The Great Depression affected many of us, but I do not recall specific hardships during that time in detail. I continued farming and trucking through the 1920s and into the 1930s. The economic challenges were part of the background as we worked hard to support our family. The trucking business lasted until around 1937 before the war broke out.

explicit_text: I do not recall direct interactions with Franklin D. Roosevelt, but his presidency overlapped with my time farming and running a trucking business in California. His policies during the Great Depression and the New Deal era influenced the agricultural and immigrant communities. We lived through his administration while building our life here.
